article.body_preview:
Agencies | Dhaka

The Daily Tribune - www.newsofbahrain.com

The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has signed off on a $4.7 billion support loan package for Bangladesh to help it cope with soaring energy and food costs that have sparked huge protests.

Bangladesh and other South Asian countries dependent on fossil fuel imports were hit hard by sharp cost-of-living increases following Russia's attack of Ukraine.

Nationwide blackouts of up to 13 hours a day hit the electricity grid last year and

llm_connections.evidence_quote: The local currency has depreciated around 25 per cent against the US dollar since last May, driving up costs for petrol distributors and power utilities
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly reports a national-level ~25% depreciation of the local currency versus the US dollar, matching the feature's focus on USD-to-BDT exchange rate volatility.
